body:
Hey @vvo for the task : 

>  there's no Algolia logo in header on small screen

If you talk about this : 
![](https://puu.sh/s1I1v/717dbde70a.png)

It's made on purpose

@vvo you mean "not animate it" ? 

> the awesome logo and animation is too slow to show, easy to miss it. I would show it right away so the experience is not too surprising if you started to read the page already

> It's made on purpose

Works for me, I just wanted to point that there's then no way to go to Algolia.com on mobile easily versus desktop.

> @vvo you mean "not animate it" ?

No, just that it seems as if there was some (long) timer before we start showing it. Sometime I already started browsing the page/reading and the logo animation starts (but it's "too late", I won't see it)

small screen glitch:

![2016-10-31-161731_1410x840_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/123822/19859464/a06ad756-9f85-11e6-93b1-f7f4011f6e27.png)

So, for the animation task : 

> The awesome logo and animation is too slow to show, easy to miss it [...]

I decided to add two variables to handle that. That way, you will be able to setup the desired animation length and delay, I already reduced the delay before the animation starts, but still, if you want to test, you can modify the value  of the variables : 

``` scss
$hero-logo-anim-length: 5s;
$hero-logo-anim-delay: 0.3s;
```

Just looking at the doc after adding all the widgets/connectors jsdoc, and the footer seems to take a lot of space making the sidebar scrolling. 

![capture d ecran 2016-11-03 a 10 33 15](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6885378/19961096/fd75d87e-a1b0-11e6-8085-df5b7754e7c0.png)

Do you have the same issue?

From my POV the current footer:
- is taking too much visual space, we are used to it but I would not be surprised people try to make sense of this area while they should not have to (your screenshot being a good example)
- is not in line with the new header

I am sure we will be able to solve that in the coming weeks once @LukyVj can work again with us. In the meantime any input from @LukyVj @AntoinePlu @Shipow is welcomed!

Huh, the footer is the community footer :/ We designed it and it's present on each and every community website, if we have to change it, we would have to change it everywhere, so that wont happen right now :/ Sorry. 

We'll do our best
Little bit more precision here: it only appears on the introduction page (if you choose an item in the sidebar, you'll get a big body). So I assume that's an issue with the template used for intro.

Oh, I see what you mean, keep this thread open, I'll fix that on monday :) \o/ 

Thanks for your reactivity!

There's also some examples that still have issues. I updated this issue with the problem. 
I'll try to understand and fix what's wrong in the meantime :)

Really ? I checked them all yesterday, I didn't spotted anything wrong, but maybe I missed them. All I know is that I fixed the community header & I fixed the airbnb demo. If you need urgent help, call me \o/
As for the footer styling, I understand it's currently common to the whole community. I hope we can still make it evolve as we updated the header, the hero sizing. "At some point" :)
Yup, it's in the `|`

Just to let you know that I'm currently fixing the examples (material-ui is the only one that's left). Examples break because of the usage of bootstrap in both the doc website and examples).
We we'll rewrite the examples soon so hopefully we will manage to reproduce those kind of issue but for now I just try to redefine bootstrap style for the examples. 
So keep in mind that if you modify a bootstrap style for the website, their's a chance it will break the examples.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/6885378/19978584/bbba3e7e-a1f7-11e6-8ed4-d740896e024d.png)

But maybe the solution here is to shorten the title :)

> But maybe the solution here is to shorten the title :)

Yes. "3rd party libraries" is enough

> when resizing the doc window in half, there's no more menu to switch between widgets/guides/etc. Also the title/logo in the header kinda disappear. Then when resizing the window to fit all the screen, the right menu is being displayed on the header.

Can't reproduce it /o\
Help ?

seems that the hamburger menu on the right is back :)
Still if you're resizing the window if half on small screen (like a macbook), there no more sidebar for guides/api... and nothing to replace it so that can be tricky to navigate inside.

For the : 

>  code snippet are not highlighted in the guide section (only css/html remaining for now)

I think @bobylito got the answer :)

@vvo @mthuret Who created that issue ? 

>  a too large value inside a prop table broke the layout (see HOC/HierarchicalMenu.html)
@vvo @LukyVj It is highlighted on my current branch 😕 Probably something was removed in the meantime. Can you check @LukyVj ?
Yup, where should I check @bobylito ?
@bobylito @LukyVj : js is highlighted, but not the css/html example (guide/styling)
Let's do another round of design issues
